body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Part 3 New Brunswick, Ontario, and Manitoba non-refundable tax credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Part 3 calculates provincial non-refundable tax credits for a multiple-jurisdiction return. For New Brunswick, the form uses a basic personal amount of $13,396 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 9.40% encoded as 0.094, and a Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate of 17.95% encoded as 0.1795. The New Brunswick age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $6,037, an income threshold of $44,945, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 uses a base amount of $11,550 and a maximum of $10,499 after the spouse or common-law partner net income reduction. The eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 uses the same $11,550 base and $10,499 maximum. New Brunswick Part 3 also includes infirm dependant amount, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, a $5,000 volunteer firefighters or search and rescue volunteers amount, pension income capped at $1,000, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, and medical expenses. The New Brunswick medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,798 from eligible medical expenses.

For Ontario, the form uses a basic personal amount of $12,747 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 5.05% encoded as 0.0505, and a Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate of 11.16% encoded as 0.1116. The Ontario age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $6,223, an income threshold of $46,330, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 uses a base amount of $11,905 and a maximum of $10,823 after the spouse or common-law partner net income reduction. The eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 uses the same $11,905 base and $10,823 maximum. Ontario Part 3 also includes the Ontario caregiver amount line 58185, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, adoption expenses capped at $15,551 per child, pension income capped at $1,762, disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, and medical expenses. The Ontario medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,885 from allowable Ontario medical expenses on line 57880.

For Manitoba, the worksheet line 58040 basic personal amount starts at $15,780, phases out starting at $200,000 of net income, and is zero at $400,000; the normalized phase-out range is $200,000. Manitoba Part 3 uses a low credit rate of 10.8% encoded as 0.108 and a Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate of 17.4% encoded as 0.174. The Manitoba age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $3,728, an income threshold of $27,749, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 and eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 use a base amount of $9,134. Manitoba Part 3 also includes infirm dependant amount, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, a $6,000 volunteer firefighters or search and rescue volunteers amount, fitness amount capped at $500, children's arts amount capped at $500 per child, adoption expenses capped at $10,000 per child, pension income capped at $1,000, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, Schedule MB428-A MJ family tax benefit line 61470, and medical expenses. The Manitoba medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $1,728 from eligible medical expenses.

For each province in this Part 3 batch, the encoded subtotal adds the basic personal amount, age amount, spouse amount, eligible dependant amount, selected listed credits, capped province-specific amounts, and disability-related lines. The total non-refundable tax credit line D multiplies the subtotal and later Part 3 lines by the province's low credit rate and adds the Schedule 9 high-rate amount from line 14 multiplied by the province's Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate. Per-dependant row tables for infirm dependants, Ontario caregivers, caregiver amounts, disability transfers, and medical expenses for other dependants, plus detailed S2 and S11 transfer schedules and Schedule MB428-A MJ family tax benefit details, are not encoded in this focused module.
